title: yosys incrorrectly infers latches
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I was trying to synthesize some VHDL designs using ghdl and yosys and discovered that there were latches inferred when I expected none. I managed to simplify the verilog produced by ghdl to this:

```verilog
module dff
  (input  clk,
   input  d,
   input  load,
   output q);
  wire r;
  wire rin;
  wire n4_o;
  reg n5_o;
  reg n11_q;
  assign q = r;
  assign r = n11_q;
  assign rin = n5_o;
  assign n4_o = load == 1'b1;
  always @*
    case (n4_o)
      1'b1: n5_o <= d;
    endcase
  always @(posedge clk)
    n11_q <= rin;
endmodule
```

This shows that latches are produced:
```shell
% yosys -p "read_verilog dff.v; synth_xilinx;"
...
   Number of cells:                  7
     BUFG                            1
     FDRE                            1
     IBUF                            3
     LDCE                            1
     OBUF                            1
...
```

Note the LDCE used here, in addition to the expected FDRE.

Strangely, this does not produce any latches, ~~and can be used as workaround~~:

```shell
% yosys -p "read_verilog dff.v; proc_dff; synth_xilinx;"
...
   Number of cells:                  7
     BUFG                            1
     FDRE                            1
     IBUF                            3
     LUT3                            1
     OBUF                            1
...
```

The problem does not seem to be specific to `synth_xilinx`, running `synth_intel` without explicit `proc_dff` results in a crash:
```
2.18. Executing DFFLEGALIZE pass (convert FFs to types supported by the target).
ERROR: FF dff.$auto$ff.cc:262:slice$95 (type $_DLATCH_P_) cannot be legalized: D latches are not supported
```

I'm not very familiar with verilog, so there may be some problems with the code produced by ghdl, but I find it strange that yosys is able to produce expected result when using `proc_dff`.
code_blocks:
% yosys -p "read_verilog dff.v; synth_xilinx;"
...
   Number of cells:                  7
     BUFG                            1
     FDRE                            1
     IBUF                            3
     LDCE                            1
     OBUF                            1
...

% yosys -p "read_verilog dff.v; proc_dff; synth_xilinx;"
...
   Number of cells:                  7
     BUFG                            1
     FDRE                            1
     IBUF                            3
     LUT3                            1
     OBUF                            1
...
